summary: Threads a `timeout` through the upload config and adds a retry wrapper. The change is focused, but the `timeout` type was widened in a way that breaks existing callers, and a couple of smaller cleanups are worth making before merge.

findings.title: timeout type widened from number to number | string
findings.description: Widening `timeout` to `number | string` breaks every call site that does arithmetic on it — those now either type-error or silently coerce.
findings.recommendation: Keep `timeout: number` and coerce string inputs at the edge with a small `parseTimeout` helper.
findings.reasoning: The diff changes the field type without updating any consumer; a grep for `.timeout` shows arithmetic use in the client, which `number | string` no longer satisfies.

findings.title: Vague comment: 'retry wrapper'
findings.description: `// retry wrapper` doesn't say what is retried or under what conditions.
findings.recommendation: Name what it retries and the backoff policy, or drop the comment.
findings.reasoning: A future reader cannot tell from the comment what the wrapper does; the retry logic it refers to is several lines above.

findings.title: Declare a package version alongside __all__
findings.description: The package exposes `__all__` but no `__version__`; downstream tooling that reads `pkg.__version__` will fail.
findings.reasoning: Pure-insertion fix — there is no line to replace, so this projects to a raw ```patch block rather than a GitHub suggestion (demonstrates the fallback).

findings.title: Retry loop has no upper bound (finding outside the diff)
findings.description: The legacy retry loop can spin forever if the endpoint stays down. Not in this diff, so it is demoted to the summary as a stray.
findings.reasoning: `old_code.ts` is unchanged by this PR, so lines 120–124 are outside every diff hunk and cannot be anchored inline — the commenter surfaces it in the sticky's strays section with its reasoning fold instead of dropping it.
